nodes.source_file: E:\CURRENT PROJECT ON WORKING\AI PHARMACY v2\AUDIT\05_PROBLEMS_AND_SOLUTIONS.md

nodes.label: Problem: SQLITE_MISUSE crashes at startup
nodes.source_file: E:\CURRENT PROJECT ON WORKING\AI PHARMACY v2\AUDIT\05_PROBLEMS_AND_SOLUTIONS.md
nodes.rationale: Two separate DB connection handles (sqlite wrapper in database.ts vs dbManager in routes) fought over the same file causing internal state corruption; fixed via singleton DatabaseManager pattern.

nodes.label: DatabaseManager Singleton pattern (src/database/connection.ts)
nodes.source_file: E:\CURRENT PROJECT ON WORKING\AI PHARMACY v2\AUDIT\05_PROBLEMS_AND_SOLUTIONS.md

nodes.label: Problem: Background worker crashes kill main server
nodes.source_file: E:\CURRENT PROJECT ON WORKING\AI PHARMACY v2\AUDIT\05_PROBLEMS_AND_SOLUTIONS.md

nodes.label: AUDIT 06: Lightweight & RAM Efficiency Analysis
nodes.source_file: E:\CURRENT PROJECT ON WORKING\AI PHARMACY v2\AUDIT\06_PERFORMANCE_AND_RAM.md

nodes.label: SQLite Zero-Process Database rationale
nodes.source_file: E:\CURRENT PROJECT ON WORKING\AI PHARMACY v2\AUDIT\06_PERFORMANCE_AND_RAM.md
nodes.rationale: SQLite runs embedded in the app process (no separate DB/cache/queue server), keeping total RAM ~80-120MB vs 350-700MB+ for a traditional Postgres+Redis+RabbitMQ stack.
